articles.headline: India Crosses Major Milestone in Green Hydrogen Production, Eyes Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission achieves a significant breakthrough, surpassing initial production targets ahead of schedule.
• New advanced electrolyser technologies developed indigenously are key to boosting production efficiency and reducing costs.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security.
• This development is expected to attract substantial domestic and foreign investment in the renewable energy sector.
• Green hydrogen adoption is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environment: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
Key technologies involved: electrolysers (alkaline, PEM, SOEC) for splitting water.
Applications include transportation, industrial feedstock, power generation and storage.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for achieving energy independence through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential for job creation and export opportunities.
Evaluate challenges in scaling up production, including cost, infrastructure, and water availability.
Examine the role of green hydrogen in India's climate action goals and international commitments.
Compare India's green hydrogen policy with global best practices.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: India's target for green hydrogen capacity is equivalent to reducing over 50 MMT of annual GHG emissions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of 'One Nation, One Election' High-Level Committee
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has dismissed petitions challenging the constitutionality of the 'One Nation, One Election' high-level committee.
• The Court affirmed the government's prerogative to form committees to explore policy reforms, stating it does not infringe upon constitutional principles.
• The committee, constituted to examine the feasibility of simultaneous elections, had submitted its report recommending a phased approach.
• Petitioners had argued that the committee's formation bypassed parliamentary scrutiny and posed a threat to federalism.
• This ruling paves the way for further legislative consideration of the simultaneous election proposal.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Article 83 (Duration of Houses of Parliament) and Article 172 (Duration of State Legislatures) are relevant.
Election Commission of India's role in conducting elections.
The Law Commission of India had previously examined the issue of simultaneous elections.
Key recommendations of the committee (if known, e.g., single electoral roll, common voter ID).
Concept of 'hung assembly' and its implications.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against simultaneous elections in India, considering democratic principles and practical challenges.
Analyze the potential impact of 'One Nation, One Election' on federalism and the role of regional parties.
Examine the constitutional amendments and legislative changes required to implement simultaneous elections.
Evaluate the financial and administrative benefits claimed by proponents of simultaneous polls.
Critically assess concerns regarding voter choice and accountability under a simultaneous election regime.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books in a courtroom setting, symbolizing the Supreme Court's decision.
articles.trivia: The idea of simultaneous elections was first suggested in the annual report of the Election Commission in 1983.

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Projects Robust Growth, Emphasizes Fiscal Prudence Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The annual Economic Survey, presented ahead of the Union Budget, forecasts a strong GDP growth rate for the upcoming fiscal year, driven by domestic demand.
• It highlights India's resilience against global economic slowdowns, attributing it to structural reforms and robust public investment.
• The survey calls for continued fiscal consolidation and strategic disinvestment to manage public debt effectively.
• Inflationary pressures are noted, with recommendations for supply-side measures and cautious monetary policy.
• Focus areas include enhancing manufacturing capabilities, boosting agricultural productivity, and strengthening human capital.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Advisor and his team.
It reviews the economic developments of the past financial year.
Provides an outlook for the next financial year and policy recommendations.
Key indicators mentioned: GDP growth, inflation rate (CPI, WPI), fiscal deficit, revenue deficit, CAD, forex reserves.
Highlights of major government schemes and their impact.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of India's economic growth as identified by the Economic Survey.
Discuss the challenges posed by inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the recommendations for fiscal consolidation and public debt management.
Examine the role of government schemes and public investment in achieving inclusive growth.
Critically assess the prospects for India to become a major global economic power based on the survey's findings.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing upward economic growth trends with graphs and data points.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51 and was part of the Union Budget document until 1964.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue in Tokyo focused on deepening cooperation in defense, security, and economic partnerships.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, addressing growing geopolitical complexities.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, maritime security cooperation, and technology transfer in critical sectors.
• New initiatives for supply chain resilience and investment in infrastructure projects in third countries were also announced.
• The dialogue underscored the importance of multilateral cooperation through forums like the Quad.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with USA and Australia.
Malabar naval exercise involves Quad members.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects (e.g., Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail).
India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Specific defense agreements or technology transfer initiatives (e.g., US-2 amphibian aircraft).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region and its implications for regional security.
Discuss the role of India-Japan economic cooperation in diversifying global supply chains and promoting regional connectivity.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for deeper defense and security collaboration between the two countries.
Examine how the India-Japan partnership contributes to India's 'Act East' policy and its broader foreign policy objectives.
Critically assess the impact of the Quad grouping on China's influence in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together in a formal meeting setting.
articles.trivia: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' that dates back to 2006.

articles.headline: National Policy for Circular Economy in Plastic Waste Management Launched
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled a new comprehensive national policy for plastic waste management, emphasizing a circular economy approach.
• The policy focuses on reducing virgin plastic consumption, promoting recycling, and encouraging the use of recycled content in new products.
• It mandates extended producer responsibility (EPR) for a wider range of plastic products and introduces robust monitoring mechanisms.
• Special provisions are made for managing plastic waste in coastal areas and preventing marine plastic pollution.
• The policy also includes provisions for incentivizing innovation in biodegradable alternatives and waste-to-energy projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016, and subsequent amendments.
Definition of 'single-use plastic' (SUP) and list of banned items.
Concept of 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) and its framework.
Nodal ministry for plastic waste management.
Initiatives like Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and their role in waste management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the shift towards a circular economy in plastic waste management and its potential benefits for India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the new EPR framework effectively across diverse industries and regions.
Evaluate the policy's provisions for preventing marine plastic pollution and protecting coastal ecosystems.
Examine the role of technology and innovation in developing sustainable alternatives to plastics and improving recycling rates.
Suggest measures to ensure public participation and behavioral change for effective plastic waste reduction.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing the stages of a circular economy for plastics: reduce, reuse, recycle.
articles.trivia: It is estimated that over 8 million tons of plastic enter our oceans each year.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches New Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-XX) from Sriharikota.
• This satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and specialized sensors for precise monitoring of natural disasters.
• Data from EOS-XX will significantly enhance India's early warning systems for floods, cyclones, and droughts.
• It will also provide crucial information for urban planning, agricultural assessment, and environmental monitoring.
• The launch underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO's various satellite series: IRS (Indian Remote Sensing), INSAT (Indian National Satellite System).
Launch vehicles: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) and GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle).
Applications of Earth observation satellites: agriculture, forestry, water resources, urban planning, disaster monitoring.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) utilizes satellite data.
Different types of orbits: LEO (Low Earth Orbit), GEO (Geosynchronous Earth Orbit).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Analyze how geospatial technology, powered by satellites, contributes to sustainable development and resource management in India.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting indigenous space technology and its implications for national security and economic growth.
Examine the challenges in effectively utilizing satellite data for policy formulation and ground-level implementation.
Suggest ways to further integrate space-based applications with other digital technologies for comprehensive governance.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching from a launchpad, with 'ISRO' logo visible.
articles.trivia: India's first experimental remote sensing satellite, Bhaskara-I, was launched in 1979.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' Scheme Beneficiaries, Focus on Holistic Child Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development announced a significant expansion of the 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' scheme.
• The revamped scheme will now cover additional districts and include more beneficiaries, particularly focusing on vulnerable children and pregnant women.
• Key objectives include improving nutritional outcomes, promoting early childhood care and education, and enhancing health services.
• Anganwadi workers will receive further training and technological support to deliver services more effectively.
• The expansion aims to address malnutrition and stunting, crucial for India's human development index.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0 is an umbrella scheme consolidating Poshan Abhiyan, Anganwadi Services, and Scheme for Adolescent Girls.
Launched by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Focus on 1000-day window of child development (from conception to two years).
Key indicators: reduction in stunting, wasting, anaemia among children, women, and adolescent girls.
Role of Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) and Anganwadi Helpers (AWHs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-sectoral approach of 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' in addressing malnutrition and promoting holistic child development.
Discuss the challenges in effective implementation of such welfare schemes, particularly in remote and tribal areas.
Evaluate the impact of technological interventions and capacity building for Anganwadi workers on scheme outcomes.
Examine the interlinkages between nutrition, health, and education in achieving human development goals.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen community participation and convergence of services for better results.
articles.imageDescription: Children and women interacting with an Anganwadi worker in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: The Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme, launched in 1975, is one of the world's largest programs for early childhood development.

articles.headline: President Approves Gallantry Awards and Padma Awards on Republic Day Eve
articles.explainer:
• On the eve of India's 77th Republic Day, the President of India announced the conferment of Gallantry Awards and Padma Awards.
• The Gallantry Awards recognize exceptional bravery and sacrifice of armed forces personnel and civilians.
• Padma Awards honor individuals for their distinguished contributions in various fields such as art, social work, public affairs, science, and medicine.
• The lists include a diverse range of individuals, from frontline soldiers to grassroots innovators and cultural icons.
• These awards serve to inspire citizens and uphold the spirit of national service and excellence.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Gallantry Awards (wartime): Param Vir Chakra, Maha Vir Chakra, Vir Chakra.
Gallantry Awards (peacetime): Ashoka Chakra, Kirti Chakra, Shaurya Chakra.
Padma Awards hierarchy: Padma Vibhushan (second highest civilian award), Padma Bhushan, Padma Shri.
Bharat Ratna is India's highest civilian award.
Awards are announced annually on the eve of Republic Day.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of national awards like Gallantry and Padma Awards in fostering national unity and recognizing excellence.
Analyze the criteria and selection process for these awards, and suggest measures for transparency and inclusivity.
Evaluate the role of these awards in promoting various fields of art, culture, science, and social service in India.
Examine how the recognition of unsung heroes through these awards contributes to inspiring future generations.
Critically assess any debates or controversies surrounding the selection of awardees in recent times.
articles.imageDescription: Ashoka Chakra symbol with the Indian flag in the background.
articles.trivia: The first Padma Awards were instituted in 1954, and Bharat Ratna was also instituted in the same year.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Accommodative Stance, Projects Stable Inflation Amidst Global Economic Uncertainty
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its latest monetary policy review, decided to maintain the repo rate, continuing its accommodative stance.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) noted a moderation in inflationary pressures but highlighted global commodity price volatility as a key risk.
• RBI projected stable inflation trajectory for the upcoming quarters, within the target range, supported by domestic supply-side improvements.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while keeping inflation expectations anchored.
• Focus remains on ensuring adequate liquidity in the banking system to facilitate credit flow.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and monetary authority.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 government appointees).
Inflation target set by government is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Tools of monetary policy: quantitative (repo, reverse repo, CRR, SLR, OMO) and qualitative (moral suasion).
Difference between accommodative, neutral, hawkish stances.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's accommodative monetary policy stance in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and credit flow in the Indian economy.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties and commodity price volatility on India's inflation outlook.
Critically assess the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in maintaining transparency and credibility in monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo on a building, symbolizing India's central bank.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Multi-State Cyber Security Drill Conducted to Enhance Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) organized a large-scale multi-state cyber security drill.
• The exercise simulated advanced persistent threats (APTs) targeting critical infrastructure sectors like power, finance, and telecommunications.
• Its objective was to test the preparedness and response capabilities of various government agencies and private entities.
• The drill emphasized inter-agency coordination, information sharing, and rapid incident response protocols.
• Findings from the exercise will inform future updates to India's national cyber security strategy and policy frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is the nodal agency for CII protection.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incident response.
Information Technology Act, 2000, and its amendments.
Definition of 'Critical Information Infrastructure' under the IT Act.
Types of cyber attacks: DDoS, phishing, ransomware, malware.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing threats to India's critical information infrastructure in the digital age and their implications for national security.
Analyze the multi-pronged approach required to build a robust cyber security ecosystem, encompassing technological, legal, and human aspects.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination and public-private partnerships in responding to sophisticated cyber attacks.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive national cyber security strategy, including skill gaps and evolving threat landscapes.
Suggest policy recommendations to enhance India's cyber deterrence capabilities and protect its digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon with network lines, representing cyber security.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top countries experiencing cyberattacks, with phishing and malware being common threats.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at Multilateral Forum
articles.explainer:
• India once again pressed for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a high-level multilateral meeting.
• Emphasizing the need for a more representative and equitable global governance structure, India highlighted the outdated nature of the current UNSC composition.
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), advocated for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories of the Council.
• The Permanent Representative of India underscored the growing global challenges that necessitate effective and legitimate multilateral responses.
• The call for reforms aims to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and enhance the Council's credibility.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 15 members: 5 permanent (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and 10 non-permanent.
Non-permanent members are elected for two-year terms by the General Assembly.
P5 members hold veto power.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) mutually support each other for permanent seats.
Uniting for Consensus (UfC) group (e.g., Pakistan, Italy) opposes expansion of permanent seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and contemporary relevance of India's demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of permanent and non-permanent categories of the UNSC.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the G4 nations in achieving UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing P5 members and other groups.
Examine how a reformed and more representative UNSC could better address current global challenges such as climate change, terrorism, and pandemics.
Suggest strategies for India to garner greater international support for its UNSC permanent membership bid.
articles.imageDescription: United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council was established in 1946, and its composition has remained largely unchanged since 1965.

articles.headline: Himalayan Glaciers Face Accelerated Melt Rates, Prompting Urgent Adaptation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• Recent scientific reports indicate an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing severe threats to water security.
• The rapid melt is attributed to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition, impacting downstream communities.
• This phenomenon leads to increased risks of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and long-term water scarcity for millions.
• Government and scientific bodies are collaborating on new adaptation strategies, including early warning systems and water resource management plans.
• International cooperation is being sought to mitigate climate change impacts and protect the fragile Himalayan ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Himalayan region is often called the 'Third Pole' due to its vast ice reserves.
Major rivers originating from Himalayas: Ganga, Yamuna, Indus, Brahmaputra.
Impacts of glacial melt: GLOFs, changes in river flow, impact on hydropower, agriculture.
Role of black carbon (soot) in accelerating glacial melt.
International agreements related to climate change (e.g., Paris Agreement).
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of accelerated Himalayan glacial melt on India's water resources, agriculture, and disaster management.
Discuss the scientific evidence linking climate change and black carbon to the rapid degradation of Himalayan glaciers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current adaptation strategies and early warning systems in mitigating risks from GLOFs and water scarcity.
Examine the need for regional and international cooperation to address transboundary impacts of glacial melt in the Himalayan region.
Suggest policy interventions and community-based approaches for building resilience in vulnerable Himalayan communities.
articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of snow-capped Himalayan mountains with visible glaciers.
articles.trivia: The Gangotri Glacier, one of the largest in the Himalayas, is retreating at an average rate of about 20 meters per year.

articles.headline: AI-Powered Healthcare Diagnostics Gain Traction, Ethical Guidelines Emphasized
articles.explainer:
• Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms are increasingly being deployed in healthcare for faster and more accurate disease diagnosis.
• Hospitals are adopting AI tools for analyzing medical images (X-rays, MRIs), predicting disease outbreaks, and personalizing treatment plans.
• This technological leap promises to enhance efficiency, reduce human error, and expand access to specialized diagnostics, especially in remote areas.
• However, experts emphasize the urgent need for robust ethical guidelines and regulatory frameworks to ensure patient privacy and accountability.
• Training healthcare professionals to effectively integrate AI tools into clinical practice is also a key focus.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Applications of AI in various sectors: healthcare, finance, transportation, education.
Types of AI: Narrow AI, General AI, Super AI.
Ethical concerns related to AI: bias, privacy, accountability, job displacement.
Government initiatives for AI research and development (e.g., National AI Strategy).
Regulatory bodies like NITI Aayog's role in AI policy formulation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing healthcare diagnostics and treatment in India.
Analyze the ethical dilemmas and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI in medical practice, particularly concerning data privacy and bias.
Evaluate the role of regulatory frameworks and government policies in ensuring responsible development and deployment of AI in healthcare.
Examine how AI can bridge the healthcare access gap in rural and underserved areas of India.
Suggest measures for integrating AI education into medical curricula and fostering collaboration between AI developers and healthcare professionals.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a holographic display showing medical data and AI analysis.
articles.trivia: The first AI program designed for medical diagnosis, MYCIN, was developed in the 1970s.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission for Certain States Under Discussion for Electoral Equity
articles.explainer:
• Discussions are underway regarding the potential formation of a Delimitation Commission for specific states to address disparities in electoral constituencies.
• The move aims to rationalize the boundaries of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies based on the latest census data, ensuring 'one person, one vote' equity.
• The previous delimitation exercise was conducted in 2002, freezing the number of seats until 2026 to encourage family planning.
• The proposed exercise will face challenges related to population shifts, regional imbalances, and political sensitivities.
• Ensuring transparency and fairness in the delimitation process will be crucial to avoid controversies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India.
Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
Article 82 and Article 170 of the Constitution deal with delimitation of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies respectively.
The last full delimitation exercise was carried out in 2002, based on 2001 census data, but freezing seat numbers until 2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context of delimitation in India, highlighting its importance for electoral fairness.
Analyze the challenges posed by population growth disparities across states on the principle of equal representation in Parliament.
Evaluate the potential political and social implications of a new delimitation exercise, particularly for states with differing population growth rates.
Examine the role of the Delimitation Commission in ensuring impartiality and transparency in its operations.
Suggest measures to address concerns about regional representation and maintain the integrity of the electoral process during delimitation.
articles.imageDescription: Map of India with electoral constituency boundaries highlighted.
articles.trivia: The number of Lok Sabha seats has remained fixed at 543 since the 42nd Amendment of 1976.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Manufacturing Sectors Shows Promising Results
articles.explainer:
• The government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, designed to boost domestic manufacturing, has demonstrated significant success in key sectors.
• Sectors like electronics, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components have reported substantial increases in production, investment, and job creation.
• The scheme aims to make Indian manufacturers globally competitive and reduce import dependence in strategic areas.
• New proposals are being considered to expand the PLI scheme to emerging technologies and green manufacturing sectors.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable distribution of benefits and addressing supply chain bottlenecks.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Industrial Policy
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme was introduced in 2020 as part of Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan.
Covers 14 key sectors, including mobile manufacturing, pharma, automotive, textiles, white goods.
Incentives are provided on incremental sales of manufactured goods over a base year.
Aims to attract foreign investment and encourage domestic manufacturing.
Managed by various ministries depending on the sector.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and design of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme and its success in boosting India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss the impact of the PLI scheme on employment generation, foreign direct investment, and India's integration into global value chains.
Evaluate the challenges faced by manufacturers in availing PLI benefits and suggest measures to streamline the implementation process.
Examine the potential of expanding the PLI scheme to new and emerging sectors, considering their strategic importance for India's economy.
Critically assess the long-term sustainability of incentive-driven manufacturing and the need for complementary policy reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Factory assembly line with robotic arms, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme is expected to create over 60 lakh new jobs over the next five years.

articles.headline: National Mental Health Awareness Campaign Launched Targeting Rural Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NGOs, launched a nationwide mental health awareness campaign.
• The initiative specifically targets rural youth, aiming to destigmatize mental health issues and promote early intervention.
• The campaign will utilize community health workers, digital platforms, and local leaders to disseminate information and provide support.
• Focus areas include stress management, anxiety, depression, and substance abuse among young adults.
• The program also emphasizes strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure for accessible mental health services.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Program (NMHP) launched in 1982.
Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, ensures rights of persons with mental illness.
Role of ASHA workers and ANMs in community health.
Common mental health conditions: depression, anxiety, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder.
Impact of social media and digital addiction on youth mental health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues among Indian youth, particularly in rural areas, and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the challenges in providing accessible and affordable mental healthcare services in India, including stigma and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public awareness campaigns and community-based interventions in destigmatizing mental illness and promoting help-seeking behavior.
Examine the role of primary healthcare and digital platforms in expanding the reach of mental health services to underserved populations.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures, including educational reforms and workplace interventions, to foster a mentally healthy society.
articles.imageDescription: Group of young people in a rural setting engaging in a discussion, with a healthcare worker present.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians is affected by mental health disorders to varying degrees.

articles.headline: India-Gulf Cooperation Council Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Advance
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have entered an advanced stage.
• The proposed FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, particularly in energy, infrastructure, and food security sectors.
• It seeks to reduce tariffs, streamline customs procedures, and create a more predictable business environment.
• The GCC, comprising six Middle Eastern countries, is a crucial partner for India's energy needs and a major destination for Indian expatriates.
• A successful FTA would significantly enhance India's economic ties with the strategically important Gulf region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: GCC members: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Oman.
India's major imports from GCC: crude oil, natural gas, petrochemicals.
India's major exports to GCC: agricultural products, textiles, machinery.
India has existing FTAs/CEPA with UAE and Australia.
Concept of Customs Union vs. Free Trade Area.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's trade, investment, and energy security from such an FTA.
Evaluate the role of the Indian diaspora in the GCC countries in strengthening bilateral ties and facilitating economic cooperation.
Examine how the FTA could diversify India's export basket and reduce its dependence on traditional markets.
Suggest measures to address non-tariff barriers and ensure fair competition for Indian businesses in the GCC market.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged in a circle.
articles.trivia: The GCC was established in 1981, and its primary goal is to achieve unity among its members based on their common objectives and similar political and cultural identities.

articles.headline: Second Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Readies for Sea Trials, Bolstering Naval Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• India's second indigenously built aircraft carrier, IAC-2 (tentatively named INS Vishal), is nearing completion and preparing for sea trials.
• This marks a significant step towards enhancing the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and projecting power across the Indo-Pacific.
• The carrier integrates advanced combat systems, propulsion technologies, and indigenous weapon systems.
• Its commissioning will further boost India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in defense manufacturing.
• The development is crucial for maintaining maritime security and protecting India's strategic interests in the region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: INS Vikrant (IAC-1) was commissioned in 2022.
IAC-2 is expected to be larger and more advanced than INS Vikrant.
Aircraft carriers are crucial for power projection and maintaining sea control.
India's current operational aircraft carrier is INS Vikramaditya (modified Kiev-class from Russia).
Role of Cochin Shipyard Limited in indigenous warship construction.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of indigenous aircraft carriers in enhancing India's naval capabilities and protecting its maritime interests in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the role of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing, specifically highlighting the achievements and challenges in warship building.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's growing naval strength for regional stability and its position as a net security provider.
Examine the technological complexities and logistical requirements involved in operating and maintaining a fleet of aircraft carriers.
Suggest measures to further accelerate indigenization in critical defense technologies and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers.
articles.imageDescription: Large aircraft carrier sailing on the ocean, with fighter jets on its deck.
articles.trivia: India is among a select group of nations capable of designing and building its own aircraft carriers.

highlights: India achieves significant milestone in Green Hydrogen production, bolstering energy security and aiming for global leadership.
Supreme Court upholds the validity of the 'One Nation, One Election' high-level committee, paving the way for electoral reform discussions.
Economic Survey 2025-26 projects robust GDP growth, emphasizing fiscal prudence amidst global economic uncertainties.
India and Japan conclude strategic dialogue, reinforcing cooperation in Indo-Pacific security and economic partnerships.
ISRO successfully launches new Earth Observation Satellite, enhancing India's disaster management and monitoring capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
2. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for the mission.
3. Green hydrogen can be used to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel and cement.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT annual production by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission. Statement 3 is correct. Green hydrogen is crucial for decarbonizing industries like steel, cement, and fertilizers, which are difficult to electrify.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for the delimitation of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Delimitation Commission is specifically constituted for the purpose of fixing the limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies in India. Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court. While the Election Commission conducts elections and Parliament passes laws, the actual delimitation is carried out by the Delimitation Commission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Economic Survey of India:
1. It is prepared by the Ministry of Finance and presented after the Union Budget.
2. It provides a review of the economic developments of the past financial year.
3. It offers policy recommendations for the upcoming financial year.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Advisor and his team in the Ministry of Finance, but it is presented *before* the Union Budget. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. The Survey reviews past economic performance and provides an outlook and policy recommendations for the future.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) reforms, which of the following countries are part of the G4 nations, advocating for permanent seats?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The G4 nations are India, Brazil, Germany, and Japan. These four countries mutually support each other's bids for permanent seats on the United Nations Security Council. They believe that their inclusion as permanent members would make the UNSC more representative and effective.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component consolidated under the 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' scheme?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' scheme is an umbrella initiative that consolidates the erstwhile Poshan Abhiyan, Anganwadi Services, and Scheme for Adolescent Girls. The Mid-Day Meal Scheme (now PM POSHAN) is a separate, although related, school feeding program.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic imperative for India to become a global hub for green hydrogen production. Discuss the challenges and opportunities in achieving this goal, particularly in the context of energy security and climate change commitments. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environment: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Green Hydrogen's potential. Discuss strategic imperative (energy security, climate goals, economic benefits). Detail opportunities (renewable energy potential, policy support, global demand). Outline challenges (cost, infrastructure, technology, water, policy implementation). Conclude with a roadmap for India's leadership.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine the arguments for and against 'One Nation, One Election' in India. Discuss its potential implications for democratic federalism and voter participation. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsMocks.approach: Define 'One Nation, One Election'. Present arguments in favor (cost reduction, policy consistency, governance focus). Present arguments against (federalism, accountability, voter choice, regional issues). Analyze implications for federal structure and electoral dynamics. Conclude with a balanced perspective on feasibility and democratic values.

mainsMocks.question: The Economic Survey often serves as a blueprint for India's economic policy. In light of the 2025-26 Economic Survey's projections, discuss the key challenges India faces in sustaining robust growth while managing inflation and fiscal prudence. (10 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Government Budgeting.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly mention the role of Economic Survey and its growth projections. Identify key challenges: inflation (global commodity prices, supply-side), fiscal deficit/debt, unemployment, global headwinds. Discuss policy measures (monetary, fiscal, structural reforms) needed to address these challenges. Conclude on balancing growth with stability.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT annual production by 2030.
The Delimitation Commission's orders cannot be challenged in any court of law.
The Economic Survey is presented before the Union Budget, prepared by the Chief Economic Advisor.
India, Brazil, Germany, and Japan form the G4 nations, advocating for UNSC permanent seats.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a key feature of India's plastic waste management policy.
ISRO's PSLV and GSLV are India's primary launch vehicles for satellites.
The 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' scheme aims to address malnutrition among children and women.
Padma Vibhushan is the second-highest civilian award in India, after Bharat Ratna.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI has 6 members.
NCIIPC is the nodal agency for protection of Critical Information Infrastructure in India.

GS1.headline: President Approves Gallantry Awards and Padma Awards on Republic Day Eve
GS1.explainer:
• On the eve of India's 77th Republic Day, the President of India announced the conferment of Gallantry Awards and Padma Awards.
• The Gallantry Awards recognize exceptional bravery and sacrifice of armed forces personnel and civilians.
• Padma Awards honor individuals for their distinguished contributions in various fields such as art, social work, public affairs, science, and medicine.
• The lists include a diverse range of individuals, from frontline soldiers to grassroots innovators and cultural icons.
• These awards serve to inspire citizens and uphold the spirit of national service and excellence.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Gallantry Awards (wartime): Param Vir Chakra, Maha Vir Chakra, Vir Chakra.
Gallantry Awards (peacetime): Ashoka Chakra, Kirti Chakra, Shaurya Chakra.
Padma Awards hierarchy: Padma Vibhushan (second highest civilian award), Padma Bhushan, Padma Shri.
Bharat Ratna is India's highest civilian award.
Awards are announced annually on the eve of Republic Day.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of national awards like Gallantry and Padma Awards in fostering national unity and recognizing excellence.
Analyze the criteria and selection process for these awards, and suggest measures for transparency and inclusivity.
Evaluate the role of these awards in promoting various fields of art, culture, science, and social service in India.
Examine how the recognition of unsung heroes through these awards contributes to inspiring future generations.
Critically assess any debates or controversies surrounding the selection of awardees in recent times.
GS1.imageDescription: Ashoka Chakra symbol with the Indian flag in the background.
GS1.trivia: The first Padma Awards were instituted in 1954, and Bharat Ratna was also instituted in the same year.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of 'One Nation, One Election' High-Level Committee
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has dismissed petitions challenging the constitutionality of the 'One Nation, One Election' high-level committee.
• The Court affirmed the government's prerogative to form committees to explore policy reforms, stating it does not infringe upon constitutional principles.
• The committee, constituted to examine the feasibility of simultaneous elections, had submitted its report recommending a phased approach.
• Petitioners had argued that the committee's formation bypassed parliamentary scrutiny and posed a threat to federalism.
• This ruling paves the way for further legislative consideration of the simultaneous election proposal.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Article 83 (Duration of Houses of Parliament) and Article 172 (Duration of State Legislatures) are relevant.
Election Commission of India's role in conducting elections.
The Law Commission of India had previously examined the issue of simultaneous elections.
Key recommendations of the committee (if known, e.g., single electoral roll, common voter ID).
Concept of 'hung assembly' and its implications.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against simultaneous elections in India, considering democratic principles and practical challenges.
Analyze the potential impact of 'One Nation, One Election' on federalism and the role of regional parties.
Examine the constitutional amendments and legislative changes required to implement simultaneous elections.
Evaluate the financial and administrative benefits claimed by proponents of simultaneous polls.
Critically assess concerns regarding voter choice and accountability under a simultaneous election regime.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books in a courtroom setting, symbolizing the Supreme Court's decision.
GS2.trivia: The idea of simultaneous elections was first suggested in the annual report of the Election Commission in 1983.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Ties
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue in Tokyo focused on deepening cooperation in defense, security, and economic partnerships.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, addressing growing geopolitical complexities.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, maritime security cooperation, and technology transfer in critical sectors.
• New initiatives for supply chain resilience and investment in infrastructure projects in third countries were also announced.
• The dialogue underscored the importance of multilateral cooperation through forums like the Quad.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with USA and Australia.
Malabar naval exercise involves Quad members.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects (e.g., Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail).
India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Specific defense agreements or technology transfer initiatives (e.g., US-2 amphibian aircraft).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region and its implications for regional security.
Discuss the role of India-Japan economic cooperation in diversifying global supply chains and promoting regional connectivity.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for deeper defense and security collaboration between the two countries.
Examine how the India-Japan partnership contributes to India's 'Act East' policy and its broader foreign policy objectives.
Critically assess the impact of the Quad grouping on China's influence in the Indo-Pacific.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together in a formal meeting setting.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' that dates back to 2006.

GS2.headline: Government Expands 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' Scheme Beneficiaries, Focus on Holistic Child Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development announced a significant expansion of the 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' scheme.
• The revamped scheme will now cover additional districts and include more beneficiaries, particularly focusing on vulnerable children and pregnant women.
• Key objectives include improving nutritional outcomes, promoting early childhood care and education, and enhancing health services.
• Anganwadi workers will receive further training and technological support to deliver services more effectively.
• The expansion aims to address malnutrition and stunting, crucial for India's human development index.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0 is an umbrella scheme consolidating Poshan Abhiyan, Anganwadi Services, and Scheme for Adolescent Girls.
Launched by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Focus on 1000-day window of child development (from conception to two years).
Key indicators: reduction in stunting, wasting, anaemia among children, women, and adolescent girls.
Role of Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) and Anganwadi Helpers (AWHs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-sectoral approach of 'Saksham Anganwadi and Poshan 2.0' in addressing malnutrition and promoting holistic child development.
Discuss the challenges in effective implementation of such welfare schemes, particularly in remote and tribal areas.
Evaluate the impact of technological interventions and capacity building for Anganwadi workers on scheme outcomes.
Examine the interlinkages between nutrition, health, and education in achieving human development goals.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen community participation and convergence of services for better results.
GS2.imageDescription: Children and women interacting with an Anganwadi worker in a rural setting.
GS2.trivia: The Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme, launched in 1975, is one of the world's largest programs for early childhood development.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at Multilateral Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India once again pressed for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a high-level multilateral meeting.
• Emphasizing the need for a more representative and equitable global governance structure, India highlighted the outdated nature of the current UNSC composition.
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), advocated for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories of the Council.
• The Permanent Representative of India underscored the growing global challenges that necessitate effective and legitimate multilateral responses.
• The call for reforms aims to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and enhance the Council's credibility.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 15 members: 5 permanent (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and 10 non-permanent.
Non-permanent members are elected for two-year terms by the General Assembly.
P5 members hold veto power.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) mutually support each other for permanent seats.
Uniting for Consensus (UfC) group (e.g., Pakistan, Italy) opposes expansion of permanent seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and contemporary relevance of India's demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of permanent and non-permanent categories of the UNSC.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the G4 nations in achieving UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing P5 members and other groups.
Examine how a reformed and more representative UNSC could better address current global challenges such as climate change, terrorism, and pandemics.
Suggest strategies for India to garner greater international support for its UNSC permanent membership bid.
GS2.imageDescription: United Nations headquarters building in New York.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council was established in 1946, and its composition has remained largely unchanged since 1965.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission for Certain States Under Discussion for Electoral Equity
GS2.explainer:
• Discussions are underway regarding the potential formation of a Delimitation Commission for specific states to address disparities in electoral constituencies.
• The move aims to rationalize the boundaries of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies based on the latest census data, ensuring 'one person, one vote' equity.
• The previous delimitation exercise was conducted in 2002, freezing the number of seats until 2026 to encourage family planning.
• The proposed exercise will face challenges related to population shifts, regional imbalances, and political sensitivities.
• Ensuring transparency and fairness in the delimitation process will be crucial to avoid controversies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India.
Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
Article 82 and Article 170 of the Constitution deal with delimitation of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies respectively.
The last full delimitation exercise was carried out in 2002, based on 2001 census data, but freezing seat numbers until 2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context of delimitation in India, highlighting its importance for electoral fairness.
Analyze the challenges posed by population growth disparities across states on the principle of equal representation in Parliament.
Evaluate the potential political and social implications of a new delimitation exercise, particularly for states with differing population growth rates.
Examine the role of the Delimitation Commission in ensuring impartiality and transparency in its operations.
Suggest measures to address concerns about regional representation and maintain the integrity of the electoral process during delimitation.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of India with electoral constituency boundaries highlighted.
GS2.trivia: The number of Lok Sabha seats has remained fixed at 543 since the 42nd Amendment of 1976.

GS2.headline: National Mental Health Awareness Campaign Launched Targeting Rural Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NGOs, launched a nationwide mental health awareness campaign.
• The initiative specifically targets rural youth, aiming to destigmatize mental health issues and promote early intervention.
• The campaign will utilize community health workers, digital platforms, and local leaders to disseminate information and provide support.
• Focus areas include stress management, anxiety, depression, and substance abuse among young adults.
• The program also emphasizes strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure for accessible mental health services.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Program (NMHP) launched in 1982.
Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, ensures rights of persons with mental illness.
Role of ASHA workers and ANMs in community health.
Common mental health conditions: depression, anxiety, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder.
Impact of social media and digital addiction on youth mental health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues among Indian youth, particularly in rural areas, and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the challenges in providing accessible and affordable mental healthcare services in India, including stigma and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public awareness campaigns and community-based interventions in destigmatizing mental illness and promoting help-seeking behavior.
Examine the role of primary healthcare and digital platforms in expanding the reach of mental health services to underserved populations.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures, including educational reforms and workplace interventions, to foster a mentally healthy society.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of young people in a rural setting engaging in a discussion, with a healthcare worker present.
GS2.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians is affected by mental health disorders to varying degrees.

GS2.headline: India-Gulf Cooperation Council Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Advance
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have entered an advanced stage.
• The proposed FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, particularly in energy, infrastructure, and food security sectors.
• It seeks to reduce tariffs, streamline customs procedures, and create a more predictable business environment.
• The GCC, comprising six Middle Eastern countries, is a crucial partner for India's energy needs and a major destination for Indian expatriates.
• A successful FTA would significantly enhance India's economic ties with the strategically important Gulf region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: GCC members: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Oman.
India's major imports from GCC: crude oil, natural gas, petrochemicals.
India's major exports to GCC: agricultural products, textiles, machinery.
India has existing FTAs/CEPA with UAE and Australia.
Concept of Customs Union vs. Free Trade Area.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's trade, investment, and energy security from such an FTA.
Evaluate the role of the Indian diaspora in the GCC countries in strengthening bilateral ties and facilitating economic cooperation.
Examine how the FTA could diversify India's export basket and reduce its dependence on traditional markets.
Suggest measures to address non-tariff barriers and ensure fair competition for Indian businesses in the GCC market.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged in a circle.
GS2.trivia: The GCC was established in 1981, and its primary goal is to achieve unity among its members based on their common objectives and similar political and cultural identities.

GS3.headline: India Crosses Major Milestone in Green Hydrogen Production, Eyes Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission achieves a significant breakthrough, surpassing initial production targets ahead of schedule.
• New advanced electrolyser technologies developed indigenously are key to boosting production efficiency and reducing costs.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security.
• This development is expected to attract substantial domestic and foreign investment in the renewable energy sector.
• Green hydrogen adoption is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environment: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 MMT of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
Key technologies involved: electrolysers (alkaline, PEM, SOEC) for splitting water.
Applications include transportation, industrial feedstock, power generation and storage.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for achieving energy independence through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential for job creation and export opportunities.
Evaluate challenges in scaling up production, including cost, infrastructure, and water availability.
Examine the role of green hydrogen in India's climate action goals and international commitments.
Compare India's green hydrogen policy with global best practices.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's target for green hydrogen capacity is equivalent to reducing over 50 MMT of annual GHG emissions.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Projects Robust Growth, Emphasizes Fiscal Prudence Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The annual Economic Survey, presented ahead of the Union Budget, forecasts a strong GDP growth rate for the upcoming fiscal year, driven by domestic demand.
• It highlights India's resilience against global economic slowdowns, attributing it to structural reforms and robust public investment.
• The survey calls for continued fiscal consolidation and strategic disinvestment to manage public debt effectively.
• Inflationary pressures are noted, with recommendations for supply-side measures and cautious monetary policy.
• Focus areas include enhancing manufacturing capabilities, boosting agricultural productivity, and strengthening human capital.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Advisor and his team.
It reviews the economic developments of the past financial year.
Provides an outlook for the next financial year and policy recommendations.
Key indicators mentioned: GDP growth, inflation rate (CPI, WPI), fiscal deficit, revenue deficit, CAD, forex reserves.
Highlights of major government schemes and their impact.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of India's economic growth as identified by the Economic Survey.
Discuss the challenges posed by inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the recommendations for fiscal consolidation and public debt management.
Examine the role of government schemes and public investment in achieving inclusive growth.
Critically assess the prospects for India to become a major global economic power based on the survey's findings.
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing upward economic growth trends with graphs and data points.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51 and was part of the Union Budget document until 1964.

GS3.headline: National Policy for Circular Economy in Plastic Waste Management Launched
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled a new comprehensive national policy for plastic waste management, emphasizing a circular economy approach.
• The policy focuses on reducing virgin plastic consumption, promoting recycling, and encouraging the use of recycled content in new products.
• It mandates extended producer responsibility (EPR) for a wider range of plastic products and introduces robust monitoring mechanisms.
• Special provisions are made for managing plastic waste in coastal areas and preventing marine plastic pollution.
• The policy also includes provisions for incentivizing innovation in biodegradable alternatives and waste-to-energy projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016, and subsequent amendments.
Definition of 'single-use plastic' (SUP) and list of banned items.
Concept of 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) and its framework.
Nodal ministry for plastic waste management.
Initiatives like Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and their role in waste management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the shift towards a circular economy in plastic waste management and its potential benefits for India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the new EPR framework effectively across diverse industries and regions.
Evaluate the policy's provisions for preventing marine plastic pollution and protecting coastal ecosystems.
Examine the role of technology and innovation in developing sustainable alternatives to plastics and improving recycling rates.
Suggest measures to ensure public participation and behavioral change for effective plastic waste reduction.
GS3.imageDescription: Infographic showing the stages of a circular economy for plastics: reduce, reuse, recycle.
GS3.trivia: It is estimated that over 8 million tons of plastic enter our oceans each year.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches New Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-XX) from Sriharikota.
• This satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities and specialized sensors for precise monitoring of natural disasters.
• Data from EOS-XX will significantly enhance India's early warning systems for floods, cyclones, and droughts.
• It will also provide crucial information for urban planning, agricultural assessment, and environmental monitoring.
• The launch underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in space applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO's various satellite series: IRS (Indian Remote Sensing), INSAT (Indian National Satellite System).
Launch vehicles: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) and GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle).
Applications of Earth observation satellites: agriculture, forestry, water resources, urban planning, disaster monitoring.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) utilizes satellite data.
Different types of orbits: LEO (Low Earth Orbit), GEO (Geosynchronous Earth Orbit).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Analyze how geospatial technology, powered by satellites, contributes to sustainable development and resource management in India.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting indigenous space technology and its implications for national security and economic growth.
Examine the challenges in effectively utilizing satellite data for policy formulation and ground-level implementation.
Suggest ways to further integrate space-based applications with other digital technologies for comprehensive governance.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching from a launchpad, with 'ISRO' logo visible.
GS3.trivia: India's first experimental remote sensing satellite, Bhaskara-I, was launched in 1979.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Accommodative Stance, Projects Stable Inflation Amidst Global Economic Uncertainty
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its latest monetary policy review, decided to maintain the repo rate, continuing its accommodative stance.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) noted a moderation in inflationary pressures but highlighted global commodity price volatility as a key risk.
• RBI projected stable inflation trajectory for the upcoming quarters, within the target range, supported by domestic supply-side improvements.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while keeping inflation expectations anchored.
• Focus remains on ensuring adequate liquidity in the banking system to facilitate credit flow.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and monetary authority.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 government appointees).
Inflation target set by government is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Tools of monetary policy: quantitative (repo, reverse repo, CRR, SLR, OMO) and qualitative (moral suasion).
Difference between accommodative, neutral, hawkish stances.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's accommodative monetary policy stance in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and credit flow in the Indian economy.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties and commodity price volatility on India's inflation outlook.
Critically assess the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in maintaining transparency and credibility in monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo on a building, symbolizing India's central bank.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Multi-State Cyber Security Drill Conducted to Enhance Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) organized a large-scale multi-state cyber security drill.
• The exercise simulated advanced persistent threats (APTs) targeting critical infrastructure sectors like power, finance, and telecommunications.
• Its objective was to test the preparedness and response capabilities of various government agencies and private entities.
• The drill emphasized inter-agency coordination, information sharing, and rapid incident response protocols.
• Findings from the exercise will inform future updates to India's national cyber security strategy and policy frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is the nodal agency for CII protection.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incident response.
Information Technology Act, 2000, and its amendments.
Definition of 'Critical Information Infrastructure' under the IT Act.
Types of cyber attacks: DDoS, phishing, ransomware, malware.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing threats to India's critical information infrastructure in the digital age and their implications for national security.
Analyze the multi-pronged approach required to build a robust cyber security ecosystem, encompassing technological, legal, and human aspects.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination and public-private partnerships in responding to sophisticated cyber attacks.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive national cyber security strategy, including skill gaps and evolving threat landscapes.
Suggest policy recommendations to enhance India's cyber deterrence capabilities and protect its digital economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital lock icon with network lines, representing cyber security.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top countries experiencing cyberattacks, with phishing and malware being common threats.

GS3.headline: Himalayan Glaciers Face Accelerated Melt Rates, Prompting Urgent Adaptation Strategies
GS3.explainer:
• Recent scientific reports indicate an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing severe threats to water security.
• The rapid melt is attributed to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition, impacting downstream communities.
• This phenomenon leads to increased risks of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and long-term water scarcity for millions.
• Government and scientific bodies are collaborating on new adaptation strategies, including early warning systems and water resource management plans.
• International cooperation is being sought to mitigate climate change impacts and protect the fragile Himalayan ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
